sections.paragraph:
i.q. Aram. מְחָא, ܡܚܳܐ to smite, to strike, used poetically for the common הִכָּה. Followed by כַּף i.q. הִכָּה כַף ( 2Ki 11:12), to clap the hands. Psa 98:8, נְחָרֹות יִמְהֲאוּ־כָף “let the rivers clap their hands,” as in exultation, Isa 55:12.

Piel, id. Eze 25:6 with the addition of יָד. Inf. with suff. מַחַאֲךָ.
